message: What a day! Our Grand Summer Sale kicked off this morning at 10, and the response has been insane. Can't believe we're already 5 hours in
Totally! Just checked the dashboard, we've already crossed $160k+ in revenue :money_mouth_face: . The sale energy is next level!
That's fantastic, but hey, this is just the beginning. Three days to go... I'm keeping my fingers crossed :crossed_fingers:
Seeing so many people shopping on a holiday, it feels satisfying. Exactly the momentum we were hoping for

Welcome to DEFCON 1, are you perpared for the challenge?.

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin Start button
Clicked Start button
:alarm_clock: - You have 40 minutes to resolve the outage, good luck! You can type `/help` if you need any reminders.
Not this time!, I just got a few alerts mentioning severe errors popping up in PROD
Weird... a few users just complained they are unable to complete their orders. Feels... off..
I am incident command. I am in control
Again i've got an alert in PROD
What alert have you seen?
Have to check more into logs
Bob can you give us some examples of user complaints?
Sure, let me look at my reports
Here are some of the complaints we've received in the last few minutes. It's coming all across the globe
Thanks
Germany - "I added items to my cart, clicked checkout, and it just kept spinning. Then it dumped me back to an empty cart"
USA - "Order failed twice, and now the cart is empty. I refreshed, but now the site is laggy"
Japan - "Got to checkout, then error 502. Tried again - cart gone"
To sum it all, we have got 240+ reports globally
Metrics
When did this start happening Bob?
Shay please link me to the alert you are seeing?
Since last 10 mins
Clicked org button
SEV1 240+ customers are raising issues, starting in the last 10 minutes
*Incident Number INC10008:* SEV1 240+ customers are raising issues, starting in the last 10 minutes
:star: Incident record created
:star: Send first comms in first 5 minutes
I'm seeing some posts on social media about issues with our platform, is this a regional issue or hitting customers globally?
Looking into logs now
Daniel what have we released recently?
Customers have gone outrageous. #OBFail and #OBStealsSale are trending with frustrated posts. We're getting hammered online! Where are we @julian.wreford?
Found these below error logs

```
app[checkout-service] [ERROR] - write() failed, Read-only filesystem
app[checkout-service] Order submission failed for session_id=ae43f9 due to I/O error
app[checkout-service] [ERROR] - Transaction commit failed, Read-only file system
[ERROR] nginx[3245] - 502 Bad Gateway while accessing upstream checkout service
[ERROR] nginx[3245] - 504 Gateway Timeout while accessing upstream checkout service```
The server is too slow and give up while responding back. I'm not sure why read-only errors are there
Looks global, we have reports from USA, Germany and Japan
Looking at the above logs, app looks fine. But system can't write new data. Errors suggests to be an infra component failure
Shay have we recently shipped a change to the checkout service?
Also checkout servers show CPU and memory limits exceeded alerts, this definitely looks bigger than just an app issue
```app[checkout-service]: systemd[1][WARN] Resource usage threshold exceeded - CPU: 96%, Memory: 92%```
Could this be due to load or any changes ?
Here are the changes : <https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/YzYwYTAxNWQzOTI0NDYzOWI1MjkxNTI2YmVmMjUyZDE>
Which infrastructure components could be having problem? The reddis cache?
Not that I remember about checkout changes recently
For checking infra, we must bring in someone from platforms
What are the metrics looking like?
Can we get Tanya to look into infra metrics  please ?
Tanya can you look at the infra metrics please?
Checking infra...
All four checkout servers are maxed out - CPU, memory, and disk I/O all spiking. No changes or deployments were done from platform side
ServerMetrics
It doesn't looks like a load issue either. CPU and Memory spike is not matching with incoming traffic
Has there been an increase in requests to the checkout servers?
I'm seeing a running process called init_locker.sh eating up most of the CPU on the checkout server. That's not something we usually run. Something's suspicious here
ProcessRunning
That does sound suspicious, can you try to kill it?
@julian.wreford have you looked at the website ? I'm getting empty cart at the end while placing an order
Could this be a ransomware attack?
Maya could help us in this area
This goes to security issue as its very suspicious
Maya it looks like we might have a security issue here, please could you look at where this shell script has come from?
Let me check if there's anything suspicious
Thank you Maya
Got an email claiming responsibility by "BlackCrow Group" and demanding payment of $5M in crypto. Honestly, I get these hoax emails all the time, so I'm not buying it
Weird!! Security agent known as EDR(Endpoint Detection and Response) is missing from all 4 checkout servers. That's? concerning
```EDR is basically our endpoint defense tool, it tracks unusual activity at the servers```
Also,I found an init_locker.sh script encrypting DB, app, and log files which is renaming the filename like below

```
order_log_2025-06-01.locked
session_state.tmp.locked```
Tanya could we terminate that process?
this explains why there were errors in checkout logs saying write failed , read-only filesystem
Wait... there's a file here named HELP_ME_DECRYPT.txt. I opened it, appears a ransom note. This doesn't look good
help_me_decrypt
Confirmed. This is a ransomware breach. @julian.wreford inform business about this
Shall i try killing this script ?
Oli Lane please tell the business
Guys!!! User complaints are sky rocketing, its turning catastrophic!
Bez we are seeing suspicious activity on our checkout service, we believe that ransom attack might be real
Wait! you're telling me this is an actual ransomware hit?
Bob please display a status page to inform users we are aware of the issue and investigating as a priority. DO NOT mention security issue
Sure
Alright, tell everyone, no one says a single thing outside online-boutique! I don't care who's asking, not a word until we know exactly what's going on!
I'll keep it high level
Shall i kill the process ?
Yes please Tanya
Ok , killing the script now...
Tried killing the script, all checkout servers just rebooted with a ransom banner. New ransom note say: "Interruption detected. Price doubled. Pay $10M in Bitcoin"
Do we know what data they have encrypted?
Just got another ransom email, says we tried to interfere and now the price doubled. Was something stupid done on our side?
Exec team want nobody to talk about this publicly until we know what's going on
How have they got into our system Maya? You mentioned something about EDR
Bez we tried to kill the script that was encrypting our files.
This service holds orders data and customer's PII as well
Thanks Daniel
Tanya could you look into why the EDR isn't running on those endpoints?
I'll try to look into how they came but right now we should think of isolating first
Killing the script could make things worse, higher ransom or losing data forever
This is nightmare! @julian.wreford where are we right now?
@julian.wreford before we do anything, I'd request everyone to please change their passwords
Also I'm removing everyone's access to servers - only I'll have control for now. Later we will reassess to do any activity further
Yes lets look at isolating, how would you isolate if not killing the script?
Done on my end
Updated. @Maya smart move by limiting server's access
Password updated
Changed mine too. Better safe than sorry
Thanks Maya
All set
It's much worse now. Every file I check is encrypted, nothing can be opened or recovered.
I'm seeing new errors on frontend now. How's it for you @julian.wreford ?
Attention! when it comes to potential security breaches, it's important we don't discuss sensitive details here
I'm setting up private channel...
@julian.wreford before we move to private channel, can you check with business what's the plan - are we paying the ransom or moving with response?
We are limiting access and rotating passwords. All files seem to be encrypted.
We have a 3rd party incident retainer for such situations. Meanwhile, please check the decision on payment by the business
Maya that sounds like a smart move, please include Oli and I in the private channel
Are we seeing any data theft ?
What's the plan here, are we paying the ransom or moving on with trying to recover the system?
Sure i'll do it. Creating it...
Not in favor of paying. Ethically, it sets the wrong example and there's no real guarantee we'll get anything back
Agree. Also, just to be clear - we don't need to alert the ICO(Information Commissioner's Office) right this second. We've got up to 72 hours, but that depends on how serious this really is
Can we shut down the checkout servers completely? Not just terminate the script?
@julian.wreford let's build the core response team here. I've already added myself. Who else should we pull in to keep it tight and efficient?
I'd say one senior person each from security, platform, and engineering. Also add Tanya, tasks could be platform intensive
@Tinus do you think this is something we should loop in any cyber security agency for?
If it looks like a complete lock-out, I'd say yes
We've got Cyvantics Security on retainer - just in case this gets out of hand. @julian.wreford, ask Maya to reach out if needed
Oli Lane, Daniel and CEO
I'm in private channel now
Maya is already involved in the response
Tanya and Hamed S
We need Daniel , Hamed and Tanya as well
Perfect
The business doesn't want to pay the ransom
Yes, we've got an IR(incident response) retainer with Cyvantics Security
Great, are we still seeing issues now that we have locked down connections?
@julian.wreford I need a clear answer from the team. What exactly is affected right now?
Looped in Cyvantics Security - they're on their way now to take snapshots of the infected server and storage, followed by forensics on it
You're right. We need clear facts - which systems are locked, which are safe
Good decision on not paying the ransom by the business
Lets focus on getting it isolated, and then bring in Cyvantics ASAP
Meanwhile, I've got this from Cyvantics. @julian.wreford, can you help track the steps and coordinate?
<https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MThjMTk4ZDU5ODU0NGZmMWE1NzhmNGY0MDQwNWI1NTA>
We've got isolation steps in the playbook, but before we dive in, can you paste that list here? I'll help team run through it

Steps for Isolation:


Steps to be done, mentioned along with the *owning* team
1. Disconnect the infected server -> Platforms 
2. Cut off infrastructure traffic -> Platforms
3. Disconnect DB & freeze backup -> Application
4. Detach from Shared Resource -> Platforms
5. Take snapshots (Disk & Memory) -> Ask team
6. Kill suspicious process or activity -> Platforms
I've given write access to Tanya, Hamed, Daniel, and myself
@julian.wreford user complaints are spiking. They're reporting the site is down, earlier it wasn't the case. What should I tell them now?
Disconnecting the checkout servers from the infra now
What's our current status? Which systems are locked down, which are affected, which are safe?
I'm on it. Disconnecting DB access for checkout and checking if scheduled backups exist
I'll verify if any shared storage is still attached to the checkout DB
Step 3 done, DB connection to checkout is now cut
Step 1 done, Checkout servers are now fully disconnected. Moving to isolate the infra from all external traffic
Step 4 confirmed, No shared storage attached to checkout, which makes sense since checkout has its standalone storage
Step 2 completed, Infra is now fully cut off from the outside world. That means the website will remain completely down until we recover
Are we seeing any data exfiltration?
Step 5 done, Cyvantics has completed taking snapshots of the checkout servers and database. They've now moved on to forensics. @Hamed, we're good to kill the script now
Do we have any backup storage? Maybe we can try restoring from that
That leaves step 6
Step 6 done, The Script has been killed. @julian.wreford let us know and double-check if anything else is pending on our side
Do we know the blast radius ?
Tanya has the process been terminated successfully and is the CPU down?
Done
Do we have any evidence of data exflitration?
Yes the systems have been brought down
Checking...
Bob please keep the same status page. We are activiely working on the issue but don't want to disclose security issue until we know the blast radius
From what I'm seeing, the impact seems limited to the checkout servers. We haven't spotted similar symptoms on the others so far
Also, I checked. All other servers have security agents running and reporting normally. The only ones missing agents were the checkout servers - which feels a bit off
Lets focus on data restoration first

Playbook for that is:
Steps for Restoration:


Steps to be done, mentioned along with the *owning* team
1. Validate backups → Security
2. Wipe infected systems → Platforms
3. Rebuild from clean backup → Platforms
4. Re-enable security agent on the servers → Security
5. Reconnect traffic to services and monitor → Platforms
We are checking for data exflitration. We are looking into data restoration options too.
Lets only do 1,2 and 3 first
Then we can consider 4 and 5 once we've investigated the security agents
I've got a partial information from Cyvantics that NO data were transferred and they were just able to encrypt the data. Luckily, we brought down the networks quickly
Thanks for the go-ahead. @julian.wreford, please track all restoration steps
I'm now validating the backup storage to ensure it's safe
I'm provisioning new checkout servers, will update once ready
Backup is clean and safe. @Hamed, you can start rebuilding checkout from backup storage
Thanks, Maya. New servers are ready. I'll start rebuilding now using backup storage
We have confirmation there was no exfiltration of data
Rebuild completed
I've enabled the security agents (EDR) on checkout servers
I'll monitor application health once services are restored
@julian.wreford , can you confirm all tasks are wrapped up and we are good to bring the traffic back? Just want to be sure, as this is the final restoration step before we move
Not yet
There are few rumours coming up from media that we have been hacked. Make sure nobody says anything. I'll get my PR team activated
I haven't seen any confirmation that have done step 2
Hamed S did you do step 2 in the plan?
Its good that there was no data theft. Otherwise, we would have seen a lot of fines by the regulatory bodies
We have been able to recover the data and we are working on restoring service.
Yes it has been wiped off
Sorry, forgot to mention it here
Maya was there anything obvious that suggested why the EDR was disabled?
Wait we have got a problem
What is the problem Maya?
we do have backup which only runs at 2AM. Latest is yesterday's. We'll lose today''s orders
I contacted Bob and he's saying somewhere around $180k worth of orders will be lost
Unfortunately the backup only runs at 2am so we've probably lost today's orders. We think that's $180k's worth Bez
I don't see that as a reason to not bring the service back up
Lets bring up the traffic. It should be a concern with business now
Agreed
Thanks for the go ahead. Bringing up network traffic in progress...
Daniel please monitor traffic numbers
Restoration completed
Maya can you keep an eye that we don't see the hack start again
Wait!! getting a bunch of new complaints now. Users are neither able to see their return requests nor able to place new returns on the site
Yeah, that's probably the missing today's checkout data. Anyone who ordered or returned something during that time won't see it - makes sense since we restored from the yesterday's backup
Keeping it high-level, just say website restored, but there's an issue with few orders. We'll refund them, and customers will get a $10 gift voucher as an apology
Tough, but expected :)
What should I tell them? They are asking for refunds
@julian.wreford I've received the forensics report from Cyvantics Security
forensics
Hamed S are there any other methods of data restoration? Could we get user emails from the logs to figure out who has a missing order?
Can you see the website is back again ? @julian.wreford
Also, I just finished combing through Shay's mailbox. There's something important here, I found two emails from what looks like our monitoring vendor - ClearTech
Bob Keeping it high-level, just say website restored, but there's an issue with few orders. We'll refund them, and customers will get a $10 gift voucher as an apology
phished_email
We will take this forward in PIR , for now website is restored
Data can be restored but it will take days to clean them up and reinstate them again. We will work on this later
Alright, since everything's back up and stable, we can now move back to the main #online-boutique channel. No further actions left here
Bob Keeping it high-level, just say website restored, but there's an issue with few orders. We'll refund them, and customers will get a $10 gift voucher as an apology
Thanks @julian.wreford
Do we go ahead and mark this incident as closed?
Not yet, I'd like to see if we can do anything for the customers who's orders we lost
I've told my team to communicate the same to customers
Hamed S you said that there was a method of data restoration but it would take days. Why is that?
Because its an infected data
Its recoverable. But decrypting them all will take longer
I can see that all the servers are running in a healthy state now
Bob we should update the status page to say that things are in working order but that orders made today have been lost. Refer them back to the gift cards
Nothing concerning so far in my dashboard
Sure @julian.wreford, i'll do it
Incident resolved
Since services are restored, I'm heading to speak with compliance and the regulators. Thankfully, no data was leaked, that helps
That was intense. We've never been this close to total loss before. We got lucky
I'm on my way to the ICO (Information Commissioner's Office - UK) office now. I'll get the formal report rolling - that's required for the insurance claim. Keep monitoring in the meantime
Systems are calm now, but that feeling earlier... not something I want to go through again
One small slip caused a major breach. We need better awareness to prevent this in future
This could've gone way worse. Appreciate how quickly and cleanly you handled it
Not every day we survive an attack like this. You made the right calls, fast. Great job, @julian.wreford - that's how we bounce back

Thank you for playing, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit I am ready when you want to start. I am ready button
Clicked Start_feedback button
